summary: Sidecars are not universally best; metadata size, native syntax and atomic movement determine when in-file annotations win.

points.text: In-file metadata cannot be accidentally left behind when a file is moved or copied by tools unaware of the sidecar convention.
A tiny one-field annotation is often clearer in frontmatter or a native code comment than in a separate JSON file.
Code-oriented metadata already understood by compilers, linters or documentation generators should normally stay in the native comment or declaration mechanism.
Fleet-wide transactions and high-volume analytics may require a registry or database, but it should be an index rebuilt from repository-local metadata rather than the only source of truth.
